mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of rapid unemployment growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion phase signals supply-side bottlenecks and impending earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle phases do not predict negative 5-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or returns are positive in > 50% of contraction months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5% force markets to price in aggressive future rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation months, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or returns are negative in > 55% of expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining retail sales signals a demand shock that rapidly erodes near-term earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint decline in GDP and retail sales does not predict negative 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals improving manufacturing capacity and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration during recovery phases fails to yield positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.
